calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brianna Mannion for San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that it saw the supplement which explains what happened with the creditor's claims and questions why it has taken 14 years. Ms. Mannion stated that there was an issue with efficiency in the office that pre-dated her, that there is no reason as to why it took so long and that she is trying her best to clean things up. Ms. Mannion also stated that the Public Administrator was appointed in 2005, that very little work was done in 2015, that it was not brought to the finish line and that she is doing her best to get it to the finish line. The Court raised the issue of the bond fee being the exact same amount as the statutory fees. Ms. Mannion stated that a lot of the numbers are calculated in an internal system, that she does not think this is common and that she does not why the bond premium amount came out to statutory fee amount. The Court raised the issue of reducing fees due to delay in administration and questions why the heirs should face full fees when they were not treated correctly by their Administrator. Ms. Mannion stated that the Court has discretion to do so. The First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is under submission. Ms. Mannion is to look into the issue of whether the bond amount is correct.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-06-2005 Granted Per Written Order
